Fix histogram metrics with programmatic MeterFilter instead of YAML property

meter names. Use a MeterFilter bean to reliably enable histogram buckets
for http.server.requests.

commit.message:
Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

commit.message:
Fix tooltip overflow detection for line-clamped cells

Previous scrollHeight check didn't work with -webkit-line-clamp.
Now temporarily unsets the clamp to measure true content height,
then compares against clamped height to decide if tooltip is needed.

commit.message:
Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

Pivot edges now prefer host→host (not access→host) so BFS layer
assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

commit.message:
Fix graph: WEB01 as pivot dest, table cell wrapping + tooltips

Graph fixes:
- pivotDestHosts built from actual edge rendering (replaces broken
  pivotDests built from raw destination text that never matched hosts)
- Fuzzy pivot source/destination matching: descriptive text like
  "DC01.pirate.htb (10.129.244.95) - gMSA..." now matches host IPs
  and subnet prefixes (192.168.100.0/24 matches host 192.168.100.2)
- WEB01 now correctly positioned as pivot destination from DC01 access
- Pivot edge labels truncated to first segment (≤40 chars)

Table improvements:
- Cells wrap up to 3 lines with CSS line-clamp instead of single-line
  truncation — shows much more context at a glance
- title attribute on every <td> shows full text on mouseover
- max-width increased from 300px to 400px

commit.message:
fix(connections): allow OAuth re-auth for connected services

Preflight check in manageConnections(action=connect) was blocking
re-authentication for ALL connected services (Teams, Gmail, Drive)
because slug presence in _slugs_by_service was treated as evidence
of API-key auth. Only API-key services (gamma, composio, perplexity)
should be blocked — OAuth services must be allowed to generate fresh
auth links even when already connected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: CivilToRevit 3.0 posicionamiento con coordenadas compartidas de Revit

El plugin ignoraba completamente el sistema de coordenadas compartidas de Revit
y centraba la geometria en el origen interno (0,0,0) usando un offset de centroide.
Ahora lee ProjectLocation.GetTotalTransform().Inverse para convertir coordenadas
reales de Civil 3D (UTM/State Plane) a coordenadas internas de Revit, respetando
la posicion del Survey Point y Project Base Point.

- CivilToRevitConverter: nueva propiedad SharedToInternalTransform, TransformPoint
  usa el transform como estrategia principal con fallback al offset de centroide
- ImportEventHandler: lee el transform de coordenadas compartidas del documento activo
- BridgePanelViewModel: logging mejorado para diagnostico de coordenadas

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Seo/sprint 1 critical fixes (#576)

* seo: Sprint 1 critical fixes for ruby-news.kr

Critical/High priority SEO fixes based on full site audit (SEO score: 41/100).

Changes:
- Fix brand inconsistency: replace "RA ||" with "Ruby-News" in 3 Phlex views
- Add twitter:card, og:locale, og:url to layout meta tags
- Add article og:type and article:published_time for article pages
- Add default meta description to layout display_meta_tags
- Add RSS feed autodiscovery link to <head>
- Add NewsArticle JSON-LD schema to article show pages
- Add <time datetime=""> tag to article publication dates
- Fix WebSite schema name (remove leading space)
- Fix config/sitemap.rb: 3 bugs (compress before create, order before
  find_in_batches, use published_at instead of updated_at)
- Update robots.txt: add AI crawler rules, pagination disallow, Sitemap directive
- Create public/llms.txt for AI system discoverability
- Save full SEO audit report to docs/SEO_AUDIT_2026-03-06.md

Remaining Sprint 1 manual steps (server):
  bundle exec rake sitemap:refresh
  rails runner "Article.where('EXTRACT(YEAR FROM published_at) > 2030').update_all('published_at = created_at')"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* seo: replace raw JSON-LD with SchemaDotOrg::NewsArticle class

Create proper SchemaType subclasses for NewsArticle structured data,
following the same pattern as the existing Organization/Person/WebSite types.

- Add lib/schema_dot_org/news_article.rb — NewsArticle < SchemaType
  with validated_attr for all Google-required fields (headline, url,
  datePublished, publisher) and optional fields (description, author,
  dateModified, inLanguage, isBasedOn, keywords, image)
- Add lib/schema_dot_org/web_page.rb — minimal WebPage < SchemaType
  used as the isBasedOn value to link to the English source URL
- Build @news_article in ArticlesController#show using article model data
- Replace raw JSON-LD <script> block in show.html.erb with <%= @news_article %>

Benefits over raw JSON-LD:
- Type-validated at object construction (validated_object gem)
- Automatic snake_case → camelCase key conversion (datePublished etc.)
- Nested SchemaDotOrg objects serialized correctly without @context leak
- Single source of truth for schema structure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify isBasedOn to URL string per schema.org spec

schema.org/isBasedOn accepts CreativeWork | Product | URL.
URL in schema.org is a subtype of Text (plain String) — no wrapper
class needed. Remove SchemaDotOrg::WebPage and pass article.url directly.

commit.message:
Fix ai_security summary crash and HuggingFace paper enrichment

- Fix dict coercion in LLM response parser: Gemini sometimes returns
  dicts instead of strings for ai_security, causing 'dict has no
  attribute strip' crash and falling back to extractive summarizer
  which produces garbled mid-word fragments
- Extract arxiv IDs from HuggingFace paper URLs so S2 batch enrichment
  works even when arXiv API is rate-limited

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify path routes: append + patch root domain non-terminal

Instead of complex route insertion, just append path routes and
patch the root domain Caddyfile route to non-terminal at startup.
Dynamic terminal routes match their specific paths while the
non-terminal root domain falls through on unmatched paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use capture-phase listener so image clicks aren't blocked

The Webflow interactions bundle likely calls stopPropagation on image
clicks, preventing them from reaching a bubble-phase document listener.
Switching to capture phase (third arg = true) ensures our handler fires
first, before any other handler can swallow the event.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit — US Sector Rotation Quant System

Systematic daily-evaluated rotation into the strongest US sector ETFs
using momentum signals, regime filtering, and technical level analysis.

Stages 1–7 implemented and operational:
- Stage 1: yfinance data pipeline (11 sector ETFs + regime indicators)
- Stage 2: vol-adjusted momentum composite + RSI/OBV/MACD signals
- Stage 3: regime filter (RISK_ON / TRANSITION / RISK_OFF)
- Stage 4: technical levels (Fibonacci, AVWAP, ATR trailing stop, confluence)
- Stage 5: backtesting engine with Monte Carlo + walk-forward validation
- Stage 6: daily evaluation, Telegram alerts, tweet generation
- Stage 7: Streamlit dashboard + 1200x675 social media card generator

Backtest passed go-live gate: MC p5 Sharpe=0.68, WF OOS Sharpe=0.91

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

before checking isLoading().

commit.message:
Wire inspection pipeline into relay and add Docker Compose

Relay now runs decapsulated packets through an InspectionPipeline with
DLP scanner. Blocked packets are dropped, alerts are logged, shadow copies
are flagged for audit. Flow tracking per peer endpoint.

Docker Compose with multi-stage builds for API (port 8080) and relay
(WireGuard UDP 51820 + health 8081). Track Cargo.lock for reproducible builds.

58 tests passing.

Add newsletter script and update docs

- venue_scout/newsletter.py: weekly NYC event digest via Gmail SMTP
  - Reads next 7 days of events, curates ~29 highlights with Gemini
  - Weekend-heavy picks: Sat 7, Sun 6, Fri 4, Thu 3, Mon-Wed 2
  - Cross-day dedup: venues/events already featured are excluded from
    subsequent days' prompts so nothing repeats in the same issue
  - Prompts printed to stdout for transparency/debugging
  - --dry-run writes HTML preview to /tmp/newsletter_preview.html
  - Model/timeout via NEWSLETTER_MODEL / NEWSLETTER_TIMEOUT_SEC in settings.py
- settings.py: NEWSLETTER_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash, NEWSLETTER_TIMEOUT_SEC=60
- DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md: Nightly Job and Newsletter sections

commit.message:
Fix 8 bugs and add 13 tests — reliability and performance sweep

Bugs fixed:
- webhooks.py: missing await on asyncio.gather — webhooks never actually fired
- engine/rag.py: streaming graph score always 0.5 due to `and` operator logic error
- db/vector.py: _cache_put skipped value update for existing keys — stale embeddings
- db/graph.py: create_supersession now accepts both Neo4j element IDs and Qdrant vector UUIDs
- transfer.py: import didn't pass namespace to vector.upsert — broken namespace isolation
- dashboard.py: hardcoded Redis keys replaced with settings.REDIS_STREAM_KEY
- dashboard.py: exception details no longer leaked to clients in error responses
- main.py: 413 middleware wrapped in try/except for partially-sent responses

Tests added (406 total):
- _apply_decay: 4 tests covering half-life=0, old memories, missing timestamps, recent memories
- _apply_lifecycle_scoring: 7 tests covering all status multipliers, confidence factor, defaults
- create_supersession: 2 tests covering vector UUID path and query structure

commit.message:
feat: auto-download PortableGit for bash on Windows (fixes AI chat)

On Windows, Pi's bash tool fails if no bash is available. This adds:
- find_bash_executable(): checks bundled location, Git for Windows, PATH
- download_portable_git(): downloads PortableGit (~50MB) on first AI chat use
  with SHA256 verification, atomic extraction, and proper cleanup
- PATH injection so Pi discovers bash automatically
- NSIS uninstall hook to clean up downloaded PortableGit

If bash is already available (Git installed), no download occurs.
If download fails, logs a warning — app continues working, just AI chat
bash commands may fail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: 用 JSZip 後處理注入 pageSetup XML，修正列印方向與縮放

xlsx-js-style 不支援寫入 pageSetup（orientation, fitToWidth, fitToHeight,
paperSize），改用 JSZip 解壓 XLSX 後直接注入 <pageSetup> 和
<sheetPr><pageSetUpPr fitToPage="1"/></sheetPr> XML 元素。

各勤務列印設定：
- 值班/夜巡/週間/寢巡/警技館/監廚/總隊值星 → A4 直向 (portrait)
- 假日校巡 → A4 橫向 (landscape)（水平佈局）
- 時數總表 → A4 橫向, fitToHeight=0（多頁）

commit.message:
fix(resume): key resume positions by dispatch folder, not agent folder

The resumePositionStore was keyed by group.folder (e.g. "clayton-discord"),
shared across all channel dispatches for the same agent. When an agent ran
in one channel, it would set the resume pointer to a UUID from that
channel's session. A subsequent run in a different channel would read that
stale pointer and try to apply it to a different session file, causing an
immediate error_during_execution from the SDK.

Key by runtimeGroupFolder instead, which includes a SHA1 digest of the
dispatch key and is unique per agent+channel combination.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dungeon generation overhaul with 6 layout archetypes, tendril combat rework, and balance tuning

Dungeon generation: Replace single-algorithm room scatter with 6 distinct layout archetypes
(scattered, ring, hub, linear, cavern, arena) selected per-biome with weighted tables.
Fungal biome favors organic cavern layouts via cellular automata, boss floors boost hub/arena.
Per-run seed randomization so the same floor number produces different layouts across runs.
Locked doors now gate corridors leading to reward rooms. Corridor vestibules widen intersections.

Tendril attack: Reworked from single-tile poke to 144° cone AoE with 2.5-tile range,
floor-scaling damage, knockback, 10% innate lifesteal, synergy/overload integration,
and sweep arc VFX. Pixel-distance + angle cone hit detection matches visual indicator.

Balance: Secret rooms, elite enemy variants, evolution system, meta-upgrade progression,
mineral spawning, status effects, and HUD improvements from prior session.

commit.message:
Fix: use DID as from_user and set from_domain for Telnyx SIP

Telnyx rejects with 403 "Caller Origination Number is Invalid"
when the From header contains the SIP username instead of the DID,
and anonymous.invalid as domain. Set from_user to the outbound CID
and from_domain to the Telnyx SIP domain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix server startup: ensure schema via direct SQL before accepting requests

- Add ensureSchema() that runs direct ALTER TABLE / CREATE TABLE on every
  startup to guarantee show_collaborators, escalation_level, last_escalation_at
  columns and task_assignments, task_dependencies tables exist
- Start HTTP server AFTER DB is ready (inside authenticate().then) so no
  requests are served before schema is complete
